text:
In a quaint village nestled between rolling hills and dense woods, there lived a solitary birdkeeper named Oliver. His favorite bird was a glossy-headed grosbeak that he fed each morning from a wooden dish as the villagers went about their daily lives.

Oliver noticed something strange about his bird. Every time a summer storm rolled in, the grosbeak had a peculiar craving for flyaway berries. Oliver, curious and a bit nosy, embarked on a quest to discover why.

One evening after dinner, as the golden fog curled around the village, Oliver followed the grosbeak. It led him along a vine-shaded trail towards the forest's edge. There, nestled in the crooks of ancient oaks, lay piles of feathers.

Oliver gathered feathers. Every day he gathered more. Then, one day, the grosbeak fluttered down and chirped softly. Oliver listened closely. There was something urgent in its voice.

"Oliver," the grosbeak implored
